channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Distinct Patterns of Mobility Recovery After Stroke Using Routine Clinical Data
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 700 stroke adults identified distinct mobility recovery trajectories during the first 180 days post-stroke using Activity Measure for Post-Acute Care (AM-PAC) Basic Mobility scores, highlighting variability beyond average patterns.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: Comparative efficacy of different joint mobilization techniques in shoulder adhesive capsulitis: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials found no significant differences in the clinical effects of various non-thrust joint mobilization techniques on objective and subjective outcomes for individuals with shoulder adhesive capsulitis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over low-load exercise (LLE) and similar effects to high-load exercise (HLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain for musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions for musculoskeletal dysfunctions between physiotherapists, resident physicians, and senior physicians in an emergency department setting.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials found that the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis (MS) remains unclear, due to a narrative synthesis without pooled effects.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study found that ActiGraph accelerometers, when placed on wrists, hips, and ankles, provide accurate stride counts compared to an instrumented treadmill in both post-stroke and healthy adults, across multiple walking speeds.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy was developed, categorized into functioning, pain, and quality of life, suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis found that the implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model led to a significant 18.5 percentage point reduction in prehabilitation delivery for lower extremity joint replacement in CJR treatment areas compared to control areas.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA/mild stroke participants found that a 6-week integrated cardiovascular rehabilitation program did not significantly improve 6-minute walk test distance or moderate-to-vigorous physical activity over 6 months compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: An editorial updates the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, emphasizing a broader vision of care encompassing prevention, individualized management, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders (EDs) and disordered eating (DE) among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying moderators associated with prevalence estimates.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: A cross-sectional study found that multimodal integration of gait, balance, and infrared thermography data significantly enhances machine-learning classification accuracy between individuals with knee osteoarthritis (KOA) and healthy controls compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved 62.8% evaluation completion and 70.9% care episode completion, with 96% of completers achieving Minimal Clinically Important Differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: A study by Mohammadhadi Mollaabbasi et al. published in “JBJS Open Access” found that early weight-bearing (EWB) and mobilization significantly improve functional outcomes, rehabilitation progress, and complication rates following surgical fixation of unstable ankle fractures.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials evaluated the efficacy of pelvic floor muscle training (PFMT) for pelvic organ prolapse (POP).
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia: a systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis | Journal of NeuroEngineering and Rehabilitation
item.description: A systematic review and Bayesian network meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of non-pharmacological interventions for lower limb motor impairment in post-stroke hemiplegia.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke, noting a lack of direct comparisons and detailed protocols between contralateral C7 nerve transfer and technology-assisted/high-dose rehabilitation for upper limb impairment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study found that sarcopenia independently increases the risk of adjacent segment degeneration (ASD) within 3 years following anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF) (HR 2.22; 95% CI, 1.34-3.67; P=0.002).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multimethod consensus process, including an in-person summit and Delphi process, generated recommendations for mental healthcare and wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors, involving 83 participants from diverse tennis organizations.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective observational study monitored 4450 athletes during the 2024 Paris Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days) and sport-specific risks, with Taekwondo and football 5-a-side showing the highest rates.
item.author: BJSM
